message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
check the app, identify the architecture, library used, coding style and identify pending actions related to audit logging.

attachment.content:
- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# TODO List

This file contains a checklist of things to do for the RTE Orchestration
project. It is intended to help guide human and AI agents:

- [x] Create project skeleton using `uv`
- [x] Add `README.md`
- [x] Add `AGENTS.md` and link `CLAUDE.md` to it
- [x] Set up `pre-commit` or `prek` to ensure code quality
- [x] Set up linting and code quality tools
  - [x] `basedpyright` for type checking
  - [x] `ruff` for general linting
  - [x] `black` for formatting
  - [x] Make sure all are added to prek where possible
- [x] Make sure `pytest` works
- [x] Add code coverage plugin for tests
- [x] Document how to use `uv` and other tools in README in case people are
  unfamiliar
- [x] Add type stubs for `boto3` since AWS does not provide them
- [x] Implement storage layer
  - [x] Add basic interface/abstract class for storage repository
  - [x] Add an example in-memory implementation to validate ideas and test with
  - [x] Add tests for in-memory implementation
  - [x] Add AWS DynamoDB implementation
    - [x] Implement converting domain object to DynamoDB request
    - [x] Implement converting DynamoDB response back to a domain object
    - [x] Implement creating entries
    - [x] Implement updating entries
    - [x] Implement getting single entries
    - [x] Implement getting all entries in a given state
    - [x] Implement creating a DynamoDB based repository from environment
      variables
  - [x] Add tests for DynamoDB implementation
    - [x] Tests for creating items
    - [x] Tests for updating items
    - [x] Tests for getting single items
    - [x] Tests for environment variables
    - [x] Tests for getting all entries in a given state
- [ ] Implement processing business logic
  - [x] Add skeleton of processing code
  - [x] Wire up storage layer type to processing code
  - [ ] Implement business logic
    - [x] Handle new erasure requests
    - [ ] Handle phase 1 erasure requests
    - [ ] Handle phase 2 erasure requests
    - [ ] Handle phase 3 erasure requests
- [ ] Add OneTrust updater
  - [x] Add unit tests for the OneTrust updater
- [ ] Update the Customer API code to use requests library instead of urllib
- [ ] Add tests for business logic
- [x] Add Lambda code for creating an item
- [x] Add Lambda code for triggering process of all items
- [x] Add audit logging
  - [x] Add audit logging implementation that uses DynamoDB
  - [x] Add audit logging calls to existing code
  - [x] Add audit logging tests
  - [ ] Add audit logging retrieval 
    - [ ] Create an API endpoint to get audit log for an erasure id
    - [ ] Create a new lambda to handle audit retrieval functionality
  - [ ] Add audit logging materialising to S3 after successful erasure
- [x] Add `.gitlab-ci.yml` and enable GitLab CI/CD
- [x] Make sure tests are run in CI/CD
- [x] Make sure tests only run in CI/CD when code changes happen
- [x] Make sure linting and other code quality tools are run as part of CI/CD
- [ ] Add AWS SAM deployment configuration
  - [x] Add Create Lambda to `template.yaml`
  - [x] Add API Gateway in front of Create Lambda (private, VPCE-restricted)
  - [x] Add Process Lambda to `template.yaml`
  - [x] Get SAM to not package the whole repo as an artifact to send to AWS
  - [x] Add `sam validate` step to CI/CD
  - [x] Add `sam build` step to CI/CD
  - [x] Add `sam deploy` steps to CI/CD
  - [ ] Fix `sam delete` step in CI/CD so it doesn't prompt for user input
- [x] Make sure AWS SAM deployment can be done from CI/CD to `dev` and `prod`
  - [x] Confirm AWS SAM deployment can be done to `dev` via CI/CD
  - [x] Confirm AWS SAM deployment can be done to `prod` via CI/CD

1	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
2	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
3	Description: >
4	  Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
5	  Originally a replacement for the Tines Orchestrator, this stack primarily
6	  consists of 2 AWS Lambdas that communicate with DynamoDB
7	
8	Parameters:
9	  Environment:
10	    Description: Environment
11	    Type: String
12	  DynamoDBTable:
13	    Description: The DynamoDB table to create new RTE requests in
14	    Type: String
15	  DynamoDBTableGSI:
16	    Description: The DynamoDB table GSI to query for RTE requests
17	    Type: String
18	  AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
19	    Description: The DynamoDB table to send audit logs to
20	    Type: String
21	  VPCEndpointId:
22	    Description: VPC Endpoint ID allowed to invoke the API
23	    Type: String
24	  DryRunMode:
25	    Description: Whether to deploy in Dry Run mode or not
26	    Type: String
27	
28	Resources:
29	  CreateServerlessAccessManager:
30	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
31	    Properties:
32	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
33	      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}'
34	      VpcExecution: "true"
35	      DatadogLogging: "true"
36	      Targets:
37	        DynamoDBTable:
38	          Type: DynamoDB
39	          Properties:
40	            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
41	        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
42	          Type: DynamoDB
43	          Properties:
44	            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"
45	
46	  CreateRequestApi:
47	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
48	    Properties:
49	      StageName: !Ref Environment
50	      OpenApiVersion: '2.0'
51	      Description: !Sub "RTE orchestrator create-request API (${Environment})"
52	      EndpointConfiguration:
53	        Type: PRIVATE
54	        VPCEndpointIds:
55	          - !Ref VPCEndpointId
56	      Auth:
57	        ApiKeyRequired: true
58	        ResourcePolicy:
59	          CustomStatements:
60	            - Effect: Allow
61	              Action: execute-api:Invoke
62	              Resource:
63	                - !Sub "arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*"
64	              Principal: '*'
65	              Condition:
66	                StringEquals:
67	                  aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VPCEndpointId
68	
69	  CreateFunction:
70	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
71	    Metadata:
72	      BuildMethod: makefile
73	    Properties:
74	      FunctionName: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
75	      CodeUri: ./
76	      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler
77	      Runtime: python3.13
78	      Tracing: Active
79	      Description: Create RTE requests
80	      MemorySize: 256
81	      Timeout: 30
82	      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
83	      VpcConfig: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
84	      Role: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
85	      Environment:
86	        Variables:
87	          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
88	          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
89	          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
90	          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
91	          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode
92	          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "create-erasure-request-${Environment}"
93	          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
94	          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
95	          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
96	      Events:
97	        CreateErasureRequest:
98	          Type: Api
99	          Properties:
100	            RestApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
101	            Path: /erasure-request
102	            Method: post
103	            Auth:
104	              ApiKeyRequired: true
105	
106	  CreateRequestApiKey:
107	    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
108	    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
109	    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
110	    Properties:
111	      Name: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-api-key-${Environment}"
112	      Description: !Sub "API key for OneTrust to invoke the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
113	      Enabled: true
114	
115	  CreateRequestUsagePlan:
116	    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
117	    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
118	    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
119	    Properties:
120	      UsagePlanName: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-usage-plan-${Environment}"
121	      Description: !Sub "Usage plan for the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
122	      ApiStages:
123	        - ApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
124	          Stage: !Ref Environment
125	
126	  CreateRequestUsagePlanKey:
127	    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
128	    Properties:
129	      KeyId: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
130	      KeyType: API_KEY
131	      UsagePlanId: !Ref CreateRequestUsagePlan
132	
133	  ProcessServerlessAccessManager:
134	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
135	    Properties:
136	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
137	      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-process-requests-${Environment}'
138	      VpcExecution: "true"
139	      DatadogLogging: "true"
140	      Targets:
141	        DynamoDBTable:
142	          Type: DynamoDB
143	          Properties:
144	            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
145	        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
146	          Type: DynamoDB
147	          Properties:
148	            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"
149	        DynamoDBIndex:
150	          Type: DynamoDB
151	          Properties:
152	            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}/index/${DynamoDBTableGSI}"
153	
154	  ProcessFunction:
155	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
156	    Metadata:
157	      BuildMethod: makefile
158	    Properties:
159	      FunctionName: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
160	      CodeUri: ./
161	      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.processor_lambda.lambda_handler
162	      Runtime: python3.13
163	      Tracing: Active
164	      Description: Process RTE requests
165	      MemorySize: 512
166	      Timeout: 900
167	      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 1
168	      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
169	      Role: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
170	      Environment:
171	        Variables:
172	          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
173	          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
174	          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
175	          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
176	          PROCESS_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS: "5000"
177	          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode
178	          CUSTOMER_API_URL: !Sub "https://customer-api-proxy-internal-eks.eu-west-1.${Environment}.hbi.systems/v1/customer/find"
179	          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "process-erasure-requests-${Environment}"
180	          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
181	          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
182	          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
183	
184	Outputs:
185	  CreateRequestApiInvokeUrl:
186	    Description: Invoke URL for the RTE create-request API
187	    Value: !Sub "https://${CreateRequestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${Environment}/erasure-request"
188	  CreateRequestApiKeyId:
189	    Description: ID of the API key required to invoke the RTE create-request API (retrieve value from API Gateway console)
190	    Value: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
191

file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: >
  Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
  Originally a replacement for the Tines Orchestrator, this stack primarily
  consists of 2 AWS Lambdas that communicate with DynamoDB

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to create new RTE requests in
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTableGSI:
    Description: The DynamoDB table GSI to query for RTE requests
    Type: String
  AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to send audit logs to
    Type: String
  VPCEndpointId:
    Description: VPC Endpoint ID allowed to invoke the API
    Type: String
  DryRunMode:
    Description: Whether to deploy in Dry Run mode or not
    Type: String

Resources:
  CreateServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"

  CreateRequestApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: !Ref Environment
      OpenApiVersion: '2.0'
      Description: !Sub "RTE orchestrator create-request API (${Environment})"
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Type: PRIVATE
        VPCEndpointIds:
          - !Ref VPCEndpointId
      Auth:
        ApiKeyRequired: true
        ResourcePolicy:
          CustomStatements:
            - Effect: Allow
              Action: execute-api:Invoke
              Resource:
                - !Sub "arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*"
              Principal: '*'
              Condition:
                StringEquals:
                  aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VPCEndpointId

  CreateFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Create RTE requests
      MemorySize: 256
      Timeout: 30
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "create-erasure-request-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
      Events:
        CreateErasureRequest:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
            Path: /erasure-request
            Method: post
            Auth:
              ApiKeyRequired: true

  CreateRequestApiKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-api-key-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "API key for OneTrust to invoke the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      Enabled: true

  CreateRequestUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-usage-plan-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "Usage plan for the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
          Stage: !Ref Environment

  CreateRequestUsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref CreateRequestUsagePlan

  ProcessServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-process-requests-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"
        DynamoDBIndex:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}/index/${DynamoDBTableGSI}"

  ProcessFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.processor_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Process RTE requests
      MemorySize: 512
      Timeout: 900
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 1
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
          PROCESS_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS: "5000"
          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode

          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "process-erasure-requests-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event

1	import logging
2	from abc import ABC
3	from datetime import datetime
4	from typing import Final
5	
6	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.as400 import AS400Notifier
7	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import AuditLogger
8	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.customer_api_client import (
9	    ALL_SITE_IDS,
10	    CustomerAPIClient,
11	    CustomerDetails,
12	)
13	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.erasure_request import (
14	    VALID_STATES,
15	    ErasureRequest,
16	    ErasureState,
17	)
18	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import RequestRepository
19	
20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21	
22	
23	class RequestProcessor:
24	    """Container for Erasure Request processing logic"""
25	
26	    PROCESS_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
27	        "new",
28	        "in-progress",
29	        "phase-1",
30	        "phase-2",
31	        "phase-3",
32	    )
33	    """The list of states (in order) that will have items processed"""
34	
35	    def __init__(
36	        self,
37	        repository: RequestRepository,
38	        audit: AuditLogger,
39	        as400: AS400Notifier,
40	        customer_api: CustomerAPIClient,
41	        dry_run: bool = False,
42	    ) -> None:
43	        self.repo: RequestRepository = repository
44	        self.audit: AuditLogger = audit
45	        self.as400: AS400Notifier = as400
46	        self.dry_run: Final[bool] = dry_run
47	        self.customer_api: CustomerAPIClient = customer_api
48	        if dry_run:
49	            logger.warning("Request Processor is in dry-run mode")
50	
51	    def _check_timeout(self, timeout: datetime | None = None) -> bool:
52	        if timeout is None:
53	            return False
54	        now: datetime = datetime.now()
55	        return now >= timeout
56	
57	    def process(self, timeout: datetime | None = None):
58	        """
59	        Process the requests with an optional timeout to bail early before.
60	
61	        When working in a time limited environment like AWS Lambda, this
62	        timeout should be set a little before the whole Lambda will expire to
63	        account for interrupting the running logic.
64	
65	        The only time an error should be raised related to the timeout is if we
66	        timeout before the process has even begun as this indicates too short a
67	        timeout.
68	        """
69	
70	        if timeout is not None:
71	            logger.info("Process triggered with timeout of %s", timeout.isoformat())
72	        else:
73	            logger.info("Process triggered without timeout")
74	
75	        # This should do the following:
76	        # - Get all requests in a non-terminal state
77	        # - process each request appropriatelty
78	        # It should do this for each different non-terminal state in turn and
79	        # be checking for time remaining in-between each to make sure that we
80	        # have enough time left for this invocation, since this could be running
81	        # in an AWS Lambda.
82	        # We can also happily continue at the next function call since we will
83	        # be running often
84	
85	        if self._check_timeout(timeout):
86	            # Timed out before we began
87	            # This isn't right so raise an error
88	            raise ValueError("We timed out before we began")
89	
90	        # NOTE: We could improve the timeout logic by timning each previous
91	        # process. We'd then use this data to estimate if we'd timeout rather
92	        # than using a safety margin
93	
94	        # Collect some stats on the process
95	        failed_request_count: int = 0
96	        successful_request_count: int = 0
97	        processed_request_count: int = 0
98	        timed_out: bool = False
99	
100	        # NOTE: When we get all those requests in a given state, we may
101	        # well end up processing the same request through multiple states
102	        # if it has been transitioned to the next AND the storage layer has
103	        # consistently updated.
104	        for state in self.PROCESS_STATES:
105	            # Stop early if we've timed out
106	            # We can process this state in another iteration
107	            if self._check_timeout(timeout):
108	                logger.info('Timed out before checking state "%s"', state)
109	                timed_out = True
110	                break
111	
112	            all_in_state = self.repo.get_all_in_state(state)
113	            logger.info('Found %d entries for state "%s"', len(all_in_state), state)
114	
115	            for request in all_in_state:
116	                # Stop processing the items if we've timed out
117	                # We can pick back up in the next iteration
118	                if self._check_timeout(timeout):
119	                    logger.info(
120	                        'Timed out while checking state "%s" before request: %s',
121	                        state,
122	                        request,
123	                    )
124	                    timed_out = True
125	                    break
126	                # Process the single erasure request
127	                # We aren't using batch operations here so that we can be
128	                # idempotent as it relates to each individual request
129	                processed_request_count += 1
130	                try:
131	                    # We do this in a try block as we don't want the whole
132	                    # process to fail if individual requests do
133	                    self.process_request(request)
134	                    successful_request_count += 1
135	                except ErasureRequestException:
136	                    logger.exception("Problem processing request %s", request.id)
137	                    failed_request_count += 1
138	
139	        # After all the processing has been completed we need to quickly do
140	        # alerts as we may have timed out
141	        if processed_request_count > 0:
142	            logger.info(
143	                "Processed %d/%d requests successfully",
144	                successful_request_count,
145	                processed_request_count,
146	            )
147	            if failed_request_count >= processed_request_count:
148	                # Raise an exception if all processing failed.
149	                # This could be due to some misconfiguration so the calling
150	                # code should be alerted
151	                raise AllProcessingFailed(failed_request_count)
152	            if timed_out:
153	                # This logging message is partly redundent but if we often
154	                # time out it might be a bad sign
155	                logger.warning("Timed out while processing")
156	            if failed_request_count > 0:
157	                logger.error("%d requests failed to process", failed_request_count)
158	        else:
159	            logger.info("No requests processed")
160	            if timed_out:
161	                logger.warning("We timed out while checking states without finding any")
162	
163	    def process_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
164	        """Process a single request"""
165	        # This is where the meat of the business logic should be
166	        logger.info("Processing request: %s", request)
167	        self.audit.log(request.id, "Request was selected for processing")
168	
169	        # Defensively align the state to a standard lower case format
170	        request_state: str = request.state.strip().lower()
171	        if request_state not in VALID_STATES:
172	            raise InvalidRequestState(request)
173	
174	        cast_request_state: ErasureState = request_state
175	        match cast_request_state:
176	            case "new":
177	                self.process_new(request)
178	            case "in-progress":
179	                self.process_in_progress(request)
180	            case "phase-1":
181	                self.process_phase1(request)
182	            case "phase-2":
183	                self.process_phase2(request)
184	            case "phase-3":
185	                self.process_phase3(request)
186	            case _:
187	                raise NotImplementedError(
188	                    f'Request processing of state "{request_state}" not implemented'
189	                )
190	
191	    @classmethod
192	    def _validate_request(
193	        cls, current_state: ErasureState, request: ErasureRequest
194	    ) -> None:
195	        """
196	        Validate a request for the given current state.
197	
198	        This will raise an exception if the request is not valid.
199	        """
200	        if request.state != current_state:
201	            raise WrongStateForProcess(request, current_state)
202	
203	    def _transition_request(
204	        self,
205	        request: ErasureRequest,
206	        new_state: ErasureState,
207	        reason: str | None = None,
208	    ) -> None:
209	        """
210	        Helper function to transition a request to a new state.
211	
212	        You can provide an optional reason that will be used in audit logging.
213	        """
214	        logger.info("Transitioning request %s to %s", request.id, new_state)
215	        request.state = new_state
216	        if self.dry_run:
217	            logger.info("DryRun: Would update repository")
218	        else:
219	            self.repo.update(request)
220	        message = f"Request was transitioned to {new_state}"
221	        if reason:
222	            message = message = " due to: " + reason
223	        if not self.dry_run:
224	            self.audit.log(request.id, message)
225	
226	    def process_new(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
227	        self._validate_request("new", request)
228	        logger.info("Processing new request %s", request.id)
229	
230	        # Check if there is an email attached to the request
231	        email: str | None = request.erasure_details.get("email")
232	
233	        if email is None or email.strip() == "":
234	            # Missing email address, this should always be present as the data
235	            # privacy team verify this in OneTrust before the request reaches
236	            # our system
237	
238	            logger.warning(
239	                "Request %s has no email so will be transitioned to need-more-detail",
240	                request.id,
241	            )
242	            # Update the request before raising an exception
243	            self._transition_request(request, "need-more-detail", "no email address")
244	            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)  # TODO: Should we raise?
245	
246	        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've started to
247	        # process a given request
248	
249	        self._transition_request(request, "in-progress")
250	
251	    def process_in_progress(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
252	        self._validate_request("in-progress", request)
253	
254	        # TODO: In the original process this "in-progress" stage did not do a
255	        # great deal
256	
257	        # Enrich the request if possible or required
258	        self.enrich_request(request)
259	
260	        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've moved to the
261	        # next phase
262	
263	        # Move to the next state
264	        self._transition_request(request, "phase-1")
265	
266	    def process_phase1(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
267	        self._validate_request("phase-1", request)
268	        logger.info("Running request %s through Phase-1", request.id)
269	        # This is the legacy system stage
270	        # For now all this should do is notify the AS400 team via both Slack
271	        # AND Microsoft Teams
272	        self.notify_as400_team(request)
273	
274	        # TODO: Update OneTrust that phase-1 was done
275	
276	        self._transition_request(request, "phase-2")
277	
278	    def process_phase2(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
279	        # This is where the core of erasure logic happened in Tines
280	        self._validate_request("phase-2", request)
281	
282	        # TODO: Update OneTrust throughout each part of phase-2
283	
284	        # TODO: Subflow 1 from Tines:
285	        # TODO: Search for the customer in All Sites using the Customer API
286	        # TODO: Submit an erasure to the Customer Erasures API
287	        # If we found the sites for a customer we include those, otherwise it
288	        # is not included
289	
290	        # TODO: Subflow 2 from Tines:
291	        # TODO: Check the Customer Erasures API Status for the Erasure Request
292	        # If it's successful we then transition to the next subflow
293	
294	        # TODO: Subflow 3 from Tines:
295	        # TODO: Search and Delete Zendesk Tickets
296	
297	        # TODO: Subflow 4 from Tines:
298	        # TODO: Search and delete Zendesk users
299	
300	        # TODO: Subflow 5 from Tines:
301	        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk tickets
302	
303	        # TODO: Subflow 6 from Tines:
304	        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk users
305	
306	        # Finally after all this we transition to the next stage
307	
308	        raise NotImplementedError("phase-2 Request processing not implemented")
309	
310	    def process_phase3(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
311	        self._validate_request("phase-3", request)
312	        # TODO: Phase-3 from Tines
313	        # This is just checking if the customer still appears in the customer
314	        # API or not
315	        # TODO: What should we do if the previous erasure states all failed?
316	
317	        # Finally transition to done
318	        raise NotImplementedError("phase-3 Request processing not implemented")
319	
320	    def enrich_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
321	        """Enrich the erasure request if needed with details from other sources"""
322	        # Enrichment could include getting more details on the customer that
323	        # will be needed by downstream erasure processes
324	
325	        if "email" not in request.erasure_details:
326	            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)
327	
328	        # We will try and find details from all customer regions
329	        enrichment_data: list[CustomerDetails] = []
330	        enrichment_data.extend(
331	            self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
332	                email=request.erasure_details["email"]
333	            )
334	        )
335	        for site_id in ALL_SITE_IDS:
336	            enrichment_data.extend(
337	                self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
338	                    email=request.erasure_details["email"], site_id=site_id
339	                )
340	            )
341	
342	        for entry in enrichment_data:
343	            # Enrich with the first found entries
344	            # If the customer has used the email in multiple regions with
345	            # different details this might enrich with weird values.
346	            # Essentially this is a best effort enrichment as email should be
347	            # enough for most systems to erase by, the extra details are just
348	            # useful for debugging purposes.
349	            if entry.first_name and "first-name" not in request.erasure_details:
350	                request.erasure_details["first-name"] = entry.first_name
351	            if entry.last_name and "last-name" not in request.erasure_details:
352	                request.erasure_details["last-name"] = entry.last_name
353	            if entry.loyalty_number and "rfl" not in request.erasure_details:
354	                request.erasure_details["rfl"] = entry.loyalty_number
355	            if entry.mobile_number and "phone-number" not in request.erasure_details:
356	                request.erasure_details["phone-number"] = entry.mobile_number
357	
358	    def notify_as400_team(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
359	        logger.info("Notifying AS400 of request %s", request.id)
360	        self.as400.notify(request)
361	
362	    def send_status_update(self, request: ErasureRequest, update: str) -> None:
363	        """Send a status update to OneTrust"""
364	        logger.info("Sending update to OneTrust for %s: %s", request.id, update)
365	        raise NotImplementedError("Sending status update to OneTrust not implemented")
366	
367	
368	class AllProcessingFailed(Exception):
369	
370	    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
371	        self.count: int = count
372	        super().__init__(f"Processing all {count} requests failed")
373	
374	
375	class ErasureRequestException(Exception, ABC):
376	    """Parent exception for Erasure Request process"""
377	
378	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, message: str) -> None:
379	        self.request: ErasureRequest = request
380	        super().__init__(message)
381	
382	
383	class InvalidRequestState(ErasureRequestException):
384	    """Exception raised when a request is not in a valid state"""
385	
386	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
387	        self.state: str = request.state
388	        super().__init__(
389	            request=request,
390	            message=f"Request {request.id} is in an invalid state: {self.state}",
391	        )
392	
393	
394	class WrongStateForProcess(ErasureRequestException):
395	    """Exception raised when a request is in the wrong state for the given process."""
396	
397	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, expected: ErasureState) -> None:
398	        self.state: str = request.state
399	        super().__init__(
400	            request=request,
401	            message=f"Invalid state on request {request.id} for process: {self.state} != {expected}",
402	        )
403	
404	
405	class MissingEmailAddress(ErasureRequestException):
406	    """Exception raised if the request is missing an email address"""
407	
408	    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
409	        super().__init__(
410	            request=request, message=f"Missing email address on request {request.id}"
411	        )
412

import logging
from abc import ABC
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Final

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.as400 import AS400Notifier
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import AuditLogger
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.customer_api_client import (
    ALL_SITE_IDS,
    CustomerAPIClient,
    CustomerDetails,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.erasure_request import (
    VALID_STATES,
    ErasureRequest,
    ErasureState,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import RequestRepository

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class RequestProcessor:
    """Container for Erasure Request processing logic"""

    PROCESS_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
        "new",
        "in-progress",
        "phase-1",
        "phase-2",
        "phase-3",
    )
    """The list of states (in order) that will have items processed"""

    def __init__(
        self,
        repository: RequestRepository,
        audit: AuditLogger,
        as400: AS400Notifier,
        customer_api: CustomerAPIClient,
        dry_run: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.repo: RequestRepository = repository
        self.audit: AuditLogger = audit
        self.as400: AS400Notifier = as400
        self.dry_run: Final[bool] = dry_run
        self.customer_api: CustomerAPIClient = customer_api
        if dry_run:
            logger.warning("Request Processor is in dry-run mode")

    def _check_timeout(self, timeout: datetime | None = None) -> bool:
        if timeout is None:
            return False
        now: datetime = datetime.now()
        return now >= timeout

    def process(self, timeout: datetime | None = None):
        """
        Process the requests with an optional timeout to bail early before.

        When working in a time limited environment like AWS Lambda, this
        timeout should be set a little before the whole Lambda will expire to
        account for interrupting the running logic.

        The only time an error should be raised related to the timeout is if we
        timeout before the process has even begun as this indicates too short a
        timeout.
        """

        if timeout is not None:
            logger.info("Process triggered with timeout of %s", timeout.isoformat())
        else:
            logger.info("Process triggered without timeout")

        # This should do the following:
        # - Get all requests in a non-terminal state
        # - process each request appropriatelty
        # It should do this for each different non-terminal state in turn and
        # be checking for time remaining in-between each to make sure that we
        # have enough time left for this invocation, since this could be running
        # in an AWS Lambda.
        # We can also happily continue at the next function call since we will
        # be running often

        if self._check_timeout(timeout):
            # Timed out before we began
            # This isn't right so raise an error
            raise ValueError("We timed out before we began")

        # NOTE: We could improve the timeout logic by timning each previous
        # process. We'd then use this data to estimate if we'd timeout rather
        # than using a safety margin

        # Collect some stats on the process
        failed_request_count: int = 0
        successful_request_count: int = 0
        processed_request_count: int = 0
        timed_out: bool = False

        # NOTE: When we get all those requests in a given state, we may
        # well end up processing the same request through multiple states
        # if it has been transitioned to the next AND the storage layer has
        # consistently updated.
        for state in self.PROCESS_STATES:
            # Stop early if we've timed out
            # We can process this state in another iteration
            if self._check_timeout(timeout):
                logger.info('Timed out before checking state "%s"', state)
                timed_out = True
                break

            all_in_state = self.repo.get_all_in_state(state)
            logger.info('Found %d entries for state "%s"', len(all_in_state), state)

            for request in all_in_state:
                # Stop processing the items if we've timed out
                # We can pick back up in the next iteration
                if self._check_timeout(timeout):
                    logger.info(
                        'Timed out while checking state "%s" before request: %s',
                        state,
                        request,
                    )
                    timed_out = True
                    break
                # Process the single erasure request
                # We aren't using batch operations here so that we can be
                # idempotent as it relates to each individual request
                processed_request_count += 1
                try:
                    # We do this in a try block as we don't want the whole
                    # process to fail if individual requests do
                    self.process_request(request)
                    successful_request_count += 1
                except ErasureRequestException:
                    logger.exception("Problem processing request %s", request.id)
                    failed_request_count += 1

        # After all the processing has been completed we need to quickly do
        # alerts as we may have timed out
        if processed_request_count > 0:
            logger.info(
                "Processed %d/%d requests successfully",
                successful_request_count,
                processed_request_count,
            )
            if failed_request_count >= processed_request_count:
                # Raise an exception if all processing failed.
                # This could be due to some misconfiguration so the calling
                # code should be alerted
                raise AllProcessingFailed(failed_request_count)
            if timed_out:
                # This logging message is partly redundent but if we often
                # time out it might be a bad sign
                logger.warning("Timed out while processing")
            if failed_request_count > 0:
                logger.error("%d requests failed to process", failed_request_count)
        else:
            logger.info("No requests processed")
            if timed_out:
                logger.warning("We timed out while checking states without finding any")

    def process_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        """Process a single request"""
        # This is where the meat of the business logic should be
        logger.info("Processing request: %s", request)
        self.audit.log(request.id, "Request was selected for processing")

        # Defensively align the state to a standard lower case format
        request_state: str = request.state.strip().lower()
        if request_state not in VALID_STATES:
            raise InvalidRequestState(request)

        cast_request_state: ErasureState = request_state
        match cast_request_state:
            case "new":
                self.process_new(request)
            case "in-progress":
                self.process_in_progress(request)
            case "phase-1":
                self.process_phase1(request)
            case "phase-2":
                self.process_phase2(request)
            case "phase-3":
                self.process_phase3(request)
            case _:
                raise NotImplementedError(
                    f'Request processing of state "{request_state}" not implemented'
                )

    @classmethod
    def _validate_request(
        cls, current_state: ErasureState, request: ErasureRequest
    ) -> None:
        """
        Validate a request for the given current state.

        This will raise an exception if the request is not valid.
        """
        if request.state != current_state:
            raise WrongStateForProcess(request, current_state)

    def _transition_request(
        self,
        request: ErasureRequest,
        new_state: ErasureState,
        reason: str | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Helper function to transition a request to a new state.

        You can provide an optional reason that will be used in audit logging.
        """
        logger.info("Transitioning request %s to %s", request.id, new_state)
        request.state = new_state
        if self.dry_run:
            logger.info("DryRun: Would update repository")
        else:
            self.repo.update(request)
        message = f"Request was transitioned to {new_state}"
        if reason:
            message = message = " due to: " + reason
        if not self.dry_run:
            self.audit.log(request.id, message)

    def process_new(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("new", request)
        logger.info("Processing new request %s", request.id)

        # Check if there is an email attached to the request
        email: str | None = request.erasure_details.get("email")

        if email is None or email.strip() == "":
            # Missing email address, this should always be present as the data
            # privacy team verify this in OneTrust before the request reaches
            # our system

            logger.warning(
                "Request %s has no email so will be transitioned to need-more-detail",
                request.id,
            )
            # Update the request before raising an exception
            self._transition_request(request, "need-more-detail", "no email address")
            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)  # TODO: Should we raise?

        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've started to
        # process a given request

        self._transition_request(request, "in-progress")

    def process_in_progress(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("in-progress", request)

        # TODO: In the original process this "in-progress" stage did not do a
        # great deal

        # Enrich the request if possible or required
        self.enrich_request(request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've moved to the
        # next phase

        # Move to the next state
        self._transition_request(request, "phase-1")

    def process_phase1(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("phase-1", request)
        logger.info("Running request %s through Phase-1", request.id)
        # This is the legacy system stage
        # For now all this should do is notify the AS400 team via both Slack
        # AND Microsoft Teams
        self.notify_as400_team(request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust that phase-1 was done

        self._transition_request(request, "phase-2")

    def process_phase2(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        # This is where the core of erasure logic happened in Tines
        self._validate_request("phase-2", request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust throughout each part of phase-2

        # TODO: Subflow 1 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search for the customer in All Sites using the Customer API
        # TODO: Submit an erasure to the Customer Erasures API
        # If we found the sites for a customer we include those, otherwise it
        # is not included

        # TODO: Subflow 2 from Tines:
        # TODO: Check the Customer Erasures API Status for the Erasure Request
        # If it's successful we then transition to the next subflow

        # TODO: Subflow 3 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search and Delete Zendesk Tickets

        # TODO: Subflow 4 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search and delete Zendesk users

        # TODO: Subflow 5 from Tines:
        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk tickets

        # TODO: Subflow 6 from Tines:
        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk users

        # Finally after all this we transition to the next stage

        raise NotImplementedError("phase-2 Request processing not implemented")

    def process_phase3(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("phase-3", request)
        # TODO: Phase-3 from Tines
        # This is just checking if the customer still appears in the customer
        # API or not
        # TODO: What should we do if the previous erasure states all failed?

        # Finally transition to done
        raise NotImplementedError("phase-3 Request processing not implemented")

    def enrich_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        """Enrich the erasure request if needed with details from other sources"""
        # Enrichment could include getting more details on the customer that
        # will be needed by downstream erasure processes

        if "email" not in request.erasure_details:
            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)

        # We will try and find details from all customer regions
        enrichment_data: list[CustomerDetails] = []
        enrichment_data.extend(
            self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
                email=request.erasure_details["email"]
            )
        )
        for site_id in ALL_SITE_IDS:
            enrichment_data.extend(
                self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
                    email=request.erasure_details["email"], site_id=site_id
                )
            )

        for entry in enrichment_data:
            # Enrich with the first found entries
            # If the customer has used the email in multiple regions with
            # different details this might enrich with weird values.
            # Essentially this is a best effort enrichment as email should be
            # enough for most systems to erase by, the extra details are just
            # useful for debugging purposes.
            if entry.first_name and "first-name" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["first-name"] = entry.first_name
            if entry.last_name and "last-name" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["last-name"] = entry.last_name
            if entry.loyalty_number and "rfl" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["rfl"] = entry.loyalty_number
            if entry.mobile_number and "phone-number" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["phone-number"] = entry.mobile_number

    def notify_as400_team(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        logger.info("Notifying AS400 of request %s", request.id)
        self.as400.notify(request)

    def send_status_update(self, request: ErasureRequest, update: str) -> None:
        """Send a status update to OneTrust"""
        logger.info("Sending update to OneTrust for %s: %s", request.id, update)
        raise NotImplementedError("Sending status update to OneTrust not implemented")


class AllProcessingFailed(Exception):

    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self.count: int = count
        super().__init__(f"Processing all {count} requests failed")


class ErasureRequestException(Exception, ABC):
    """Parent exception for Erasure Request process"""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, message: str) -> None:
        self.request: ErasureRequest = request
        super().__init__(message)


class InvalidRequestState(ErasureRequestException):
    """Exception raised when a request is not in a valid state"""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self.state: str = request.state
        super().__init__(
            request=request,
            message=f"Request {request.id} is in an invalid state: {self.state}",
        )


class WrongStateForProcess(ErasureRequestException):
    """Exception raised when a request is in the wrong state for the given process."""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest, expected: ErasureState) -> None:
        self.state: str = request.state
        super().__init__(
            request=request,
            message=f"Invalid state on request {request.id} for process: {self.state} != {expected}",
        )


class MissingEmailAddress(ErasureRequestException):
    """Exception raised if the request is missing an email address"""

    def __init__(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        super().__init__(
            request=request, message=f"Missing email address on request {request.id}"
        )

from dataclasses import dataclass, fields
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Literal, override

type ErasureState = Literal[
    "new", "in-progress", "phase-1", "phase-2", "phase-3", "done", "need-more-detail"
]
"""Valid erasure states type"""

type ErasureDetailKey = Literal[
    "first-name", "last-name", "email", "postcode", "phone-number", "rfl", "last-order"
]
"""Expected erasure details keys"""

VALID_DETAIL_KEYS: frozenset[ErasureDetailKey] = frozenset(
    (
        "first-name",
        "last-name",
        "email",
        "postcode",
        "phone-number",
        "rfl",
        "last-order",
    )
)
"""Valid keys for erasure_details"""

VALID_STATES: tuple[ErasureState, ...] = (
    "new",
    "in-progress",
    "phase-1",
    "phase-2",
    "phase-3",
    "done",
    "need-more-detail",
)
"""Valid erasure states"""


@dataclass
class ErasureRequest:
    """
    An Erasure request as submitted by a source system.

    The normal source system is OneTrust.
    """

    id: str
    """
    A unique identifier for this erasure request.
    This should come from the source system.
    """

    source: str
    """The source of this Erasure request"""

    state: ErasureState
    """The state that the request is in"""

    date_issued: datetime
    """The date (and time) that this request was issued at"""

    # TODO: Perhaps turn erasure_details into its own domain object
    erasure_details: dict[ErasureDetailKey, str]
    """
    Specific details for this erasure.

    This is a sensitive field as it will contain the likes of email addresses,
    addresses etc.

    The erasure details we normally get from OneTrust are:

    - First Name
    - Last Name
    - Email
    - Postcode

    With the optional ones being:

    - Phone Number
    - RFL Number
    - Last Order Number
    """

    # Below code attempts to omit the erasure_details from inclusing in strings
    # by mistake

    def safe_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:
        """
        Returns a dictionary version of the erasure request with personal
        details masked.
        """
        return {
            f.name: getattr(self, f.name)
            for f in fields(self)
            if f.name not in ["erasure_details"]
        }

    def unsafe_dict(self) -> dict[str, str | dict[str, str]]:
        """
        Returns a dictionary version of the erasure request with personal
        details present.
        """
        return {f.name: getattr(self, f.name) for f in fields(self)}

    @override
    def __str__(self):
        d = self.safe_dict()
        return f"ErasureRequest(id={d['id']}, source={d['source']}, state={d['state']}, date_issued={d['date_issued']})"

    @override
    def __repr__(self):
        d = self.safe_dict()
        return f"ErasureRequest(**{d})"

    @property
    def date_issued_ms(self) -> int:
        """The date and time issued as a unix timestamp (milliseconds)"""
        timestamp_ms = int(self.date_issued.timestamp() * 1000)
        return timestamp_ms

1	import logging
2	import os
3	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
4	from datetime import datetime, timezone
5	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Literal, override
6	
7	import boto3
8	
9	if TYPE_CHECKING:
10	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
11	else:
12	    DynamoDBClient = Any
13	
14	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
15	
16	
17	class AuditLogger(ABC):
18	    """Base interface for audit logging"""
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
22	        """
23	        Log an audit log message.
24	
25	        The id should be the same as an erasure requests id
26	
27	        The message can be any free text. You should avoid storing raw personal
28	        information in messages, and make use of anonymisation functions.
29	
30	        The timestamp is optional and if absent will be set to now using the
31	        UTC timezone. All datetimes given will be coereced to UTC.
32	        """
33	        raise NotImplementedError()
34	
35	
36	class NoOpAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
37	    """Audit logger implementation that does nothing"""
38	
39	    @override
40	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
41	        logger.warning("Audit message not saved anywhere: %s", message)
42	        pass
43	
44	
45	class InMemoryAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
46	    """Example implementation of audit logging by storing messages in memory"""
47	
48	    def __init__(self) -> None:
49	        super().__init__()
50	        self.memory_log: list[tuple[tuple[str, datetime], str]] = []
51	
52	    @override
53	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
54	        if timestamp is None:
55	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
56	        else:
57	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
58	        self.memory_log.append(((id, timestamp), message))
59	
60	
61	class DynamoDBAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
62	    """DynamoDB backed audit logging"""
63	
64	    ID_FIELD: Literal["id"] = "id"
65	    TIMESTAMP_FIELD: Literal["timestamp"] = "timestamp"
66	    MESSAGE_FIELD: Literal["message"] = "message"
67	
68	    def __init__(self, table_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient) -> None:
69	        super().__init__()
70	        self.table_name: str = table_name
71	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
72	
73	    @override
74	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
75	        if timestamp is None:
76	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
77	        else:
78	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
79	        item = self._create_item(id, message, timestamp)
80	        _ = self.dynamodb.put_item(
81	            TableName=self.table_name,
82	            Item=item,
83	            ReturnValues="NONE",
84	        )
85	        logger.info("Audit Message sent: %s", message)
86	
87	    @classmethod
88	    def _create_item(cls, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime):
89	        timestamp_ms: int = int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000)
90	        item = {
91	            cls.ID_FIELD: {"S": id},
92	            cls.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: {"N": str(timestamp_ms)},
93	            cls.MESSAGE_FIELD: {"S": message},
94	        }
95	        return item
96	
97	
98	DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: Literal["DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE"] = (
99	    "DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE"
100	)
101	"""Environment variable used for configuring the audit logging table"""
102	
103	
104	def get_audit_logger() -> AuditLogger:
105	    """Get a concrete audit logger via environment variables"""
106	    table_name: str | None = os.getenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE)
107	    if table_name is not None:
108	        return get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name)
109	    else:
110	        logger.warning("No Audit Logging enabled")
111	        return NoOpAuditLogger()
112	
113	
114	def get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name: str) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
115	    dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = boto3.client("dynamodb")
116	    return DynamoDBAuditLogger(table_name, dynamodb)
117

32	class InvalidRequestError(ValueError):
33	    pass
