AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: >
  Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
  Originally a replacement for the Tines Orchestrator, this stack primarily
  consists of 2 AWS Lambdas that communicate with DynamoDB

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to create new RTE requests in
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTableGSI:
    Description: The DynamoDB table GSI to query for RTE requests
    Type: String
  AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to send audit logs to
    Type: String
  VPCEndpointId:
    Description: VPC Endpoint ID allowed to invoke the API
    Type: String
  DryRunMode:
    Description: Whether to deploy in Dry Run mode or not
    Type: String

Resources:
  CreateServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"

  CreateRequestApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: !Ref Environment
      OpenApiVersion: '2.0'
      Description: !Sub "RTE orchestrator create-request API (${Environment})"
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Type: PRIVATE
        VPCEndpointIds:
          - !Ref VPCEndpointId
      Auth:
        ApiKeyRequired: true
        ResourcePolicy:
          CustomStatements:
            - Effect: Allow
              Action: execute-api:Invoke
              Resource:
                - !Sub "arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*"
              Principal: '*'
              Condition:
                StringEquals:
                  aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VPCEndpointId

  CreateFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Create RTE requests
      MemorySize: 256
      Timeout: 30
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "create-erasure-request-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
      Events:
        CreateErasureRequest:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
            Path: /erasure-request
            Method: post
            Auth:
              ApiKeyRequired: true

  CreateRequestApiKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-api-key-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "API key for OneTrust to invoke the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      Enabled: true

  CreateRequestUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-usage-plan-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "Usage plan for the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
          Stage: !Ref Environment

  CreateRequestUsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref CreateRequestUsagePlan

  ProcessServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-process-requests-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"
        DynamoDBIndex:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}/index/${DynamoDBTableGSI}"

  ProcessFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.processor_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Process RTE requests
      MemorySize: 512
      Timeout: 900
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 1
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
          PROCESS_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS: "5000"
          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode
          CUSTOMER_API_URL: !Sub "https://customer-api-proxy-internal-eks.eu-west-1.${Environment}.hbi.systems/v1/customer/find"
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "process-erasure-requests-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.

Outputs:
  CreateRequestApiInvokeUrl:
    Description: Invoke URL for the RTE create-request API
    Value: !Sub "https://${CreateRequestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${Environment}/erasure-request"
  CreateRequestApiKeyId:
    Description: ID of the API key required to invoke the RTE create-request API (retrieve value from API Gateway console)
    Value: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey

lastPrompt: implement the plan

    Type: String
  DryRunMode:
    Description: Whether to deploy in Dry Run mode or not
    Type: String

    Description: S3 bucket holding materialised audit logs (one .jsonl.gz per erasure id)

input.old_string:
Outputs:
  CreateRequestApiInvokeUrl:

input.new_string:
ReporterServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-status-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"

  ReporterS3ReadPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-reporter-s3-read-${Environment}"
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action: s3:GetObject
            Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${AuditLogS3Bucket}/${AuditLogS3Prefix}/*"
      Roles:
        - !GetAtt ReporterServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName

  ReporterFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ReporterServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Retrieve audit log for an RTE erasure request
      MemorySize: 256
      Timeout: 30
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 5
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ReporterServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt ReporterServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
          AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET: !Ref AuditLogS3Bucket
          AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX: !Ref AuditLogS3Prefix
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "status-erasure-request-${Environment}"
      Events:
        GetErasureStatus:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
            Path: /status/{id}
            Method: get
            Auth:
              ApiKeyRequired: true

Outputs:
  CreateRequestApiInvokeUrl:
    Description: Invoke URL for the RTE create-request API
    Value: !Sub "https://${CreateRequestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${Environment}/erasure-request"
  CreateRequestApiKeyId:
    Description: ID of the API key required to invoke the RTE create-request API (retrieve value from API Gateway console)
    Value: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
  GetStatusApiInvokeUrl:
    Description: Invoke URL for the GET /status/{id} API endpoint
    Value: !Sub "https://${CreateRequestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${Environment}/status/{id}"

Description: >
  Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
  Originally a replacement for the Tines Orchestrator, this stack primarily
  consists of 2 AWS Lambdas that communicate with DynamoDB

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to create new RTE requests in
    Type: String
  DynamoDBTableGSI:
    Description: The DynamoDB table GSI to query for RTE requests
    Type: String
  AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
    Description: The DynamoDB table to send audit logs to
    Type: String
  VPCEndpointId:
    Description: VPC Endpoint ID allowed to invoke the API
    Type: String
  DryRunMode:
    Description: Whether to deploy in Dry Run mode or not
    Type: String
  AuditLogS3Bucket:
    Description: S3 bucket holding materialised audit logs (one .jsonl.gz per erasure id)
    Type: String
  AuditLogS3Prefix:
    Description: Key prefix within the S3 bucket for materialised audit logs
    Type: String

Resources:
  CreateServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-create-request-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"

  CreateRequestApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: !Ref Environment
      OpenApiVersion: '2.0'
      Description: !Sub "RTE orchestrator create-request API (${Environment})"
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Type: PRIVATE
        VPCEndpointIds:
          - !Ref VPCEndpointId
      Auth:
        ApiKeyRequired: true
        ResourcePolicy:
          CustomStatements:
            - Effect: Allow
              Action: execute-api:Invoke
              Resource:
                - !Sub "arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:*"
              Principal: '*'
              Condition:
                StringEquals:
                  aws:SourceVpce: !Ref VPCEndpointId

  CreateFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.creator_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Create RTE requests
      MemorySize: 256
      Timeout: 30
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 10
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt CreateServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "create-erasure-request-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
      Events:
        CreateErasureRequest:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
            Path: /erasure-request
            Method: post
            Auth:
              ApiKeyRequired: true

  CreateRequestApiKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-api-key-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "API key for OneTrust to invoke the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      Enabled: true

  CreateRequestUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    # noinspection YamlUnresolvedReferences
    DependsOn: CreateRequestApiStage
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: !Sub "rte-orchestrator-usage-plan-${Environment}"
      Description: !Sub "Usage plan for the RTE create-request API (${Environment})"
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref CreateRequestApi
          Stage: !Ref Environment

  CreateRequestUsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref CreateRequestUsagePlan

  ProcessServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-rte-orchestrator-process-requests-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: "true"
      DatadogLogging: "true"
      Targets:
        DynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}"
        AuditLogDynamoDBTable:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${AuditLogDynamoDBTable}"
        DynamoDBIndex:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Sub "arn:aws:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${DynamoDBTable}/index/${DynamoDBTableGSI}"

  ProcessFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Metadata:
      BuildMethod: makefile
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      CodeUri: ./
      Handler: dns_de_rte_orchestrator.processor_lambda.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      Tracing: Active
      Description: Process RTE requests
      MemorySize: 512
      Timeout: 900
      ReservedConcurrentExecutions: 1
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Role: !GetAtt ProcessServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          REPOSITORY_TYPE: "dynamodb"
          DYNAMO_DB_TABLE: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          DYNAMO_DB_INDEX: !Ref DynamoDBTableGSI
          DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: !Ref AuditLogDynamoDBTable
          PROCESS_SAFETY_MARGIN_MS: "5000"
          DRY_RUN_MODE: !Ref DryRunMode
          CUSTOMER_API_URL: !Sub "https://customer-api-proxy-internal-eks.eu-west-1.${Environment}.hbi.systems/v1/customer/find"
          POWERTOOLS_SERVICE_NAME: !Sub "process-erasure-requests-${Environment}"
          # POWERTOOLS_LOGGER_LOG_EVENT must NOT be set — the incoming event
          # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
          # should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.

Outputs:
  CreateRequestApiInvokeUrl:
    Description: Invoke URL for the RTE create-request API
    Value: !Sub "https://${CreateRequestApi}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/${Environment}/erasure-request"
  CreateRequestApiKeyId:
    Description: ID of the API key required to invoke the RTE create-request API (retrieve value from API Gateway console)
    Value: !Ref CreateRequestApiKey

lines: # contains erasure_details (PII: email, name, postcode), which
# should not be persisted in CloudWatch logs.
+      Description: Retrieve audit log for an RTE erasure request
Description: Invoke URL for the RTE create-request API
Description: ID of the API key required to invoke the RTE create-request API (retrieve value from API Gateway console)
+    Description: Invoke URL for the GET /status/{id} API endpoint

# This is a Makefile for building packages to deploy to AWS with AWS SAM
.DEFAULT_GOAL := help

.PHONY: help clean build-CreateFunction build-ProcessFunction

help: ## Show help
	@echo "Usage: make [target]"
	@echo "This should be run by AWS SAM"

build-CreateFunction:
	uv export --no-dev --format requirements-txt -o .aws-sam-requirements.txt
	uv pip install --python 3.13 --target "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/" -r .aws-sam-requirements.txt
	cp -r src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/"
	rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt

build-ProcessFunction:
	uv export --no-dev --format requirements-txt -o .aws-sam-requirements.txt
	uv pip install --python 3.13 --target "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/" -r .aws-sam-requirements.txt
	cp -r src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator "${ARTIFACTS_DIR}/"
	rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt

clean:
	rm -f .aws-sam-requirements.txt

2	from typing import cast

7	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
8	    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
9	    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
10	    InMemoryAuditLogger,
11	    NoOpAuditLogger,
12	    get_audit_logger,
13	    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
14	)
15	
16	
17	class StubDynamoDBClient:
18	    def __init__(self) -> None:
19	        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
20	
21	    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
22	        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
23	        return {}
24	
25	
26	def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
27	    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
28	) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
29	    assert table_name == "audit-table"
30	    return expected
31	
32	
33	def stub_boto3_client(
34	    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
35	) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
36	    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
37	    return client
38	
39	
40	def test_no_op_log():
41	    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
42	    audit.log("id", "message")
43	
44	
45	def test_in_memory_log():
46	    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
47	    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
48	
49	    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0
50	
51	    id = "foobar"
52	    message = "test message1"
53	    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
54	
55	    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
56	    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
57	    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)
58	
59	    message = "message 2"
60	    audit.log(id, message)
61	    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
62	    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
63	    # make sure the timestamp exists and is sensible
64	    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
65	    # Assert the entries are what we expect
66	    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
67	    assert found_entry[1] == message
68	
69	
70	def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
71	    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
72	    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0
73	
74	    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
75	    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
76	    id = "baz"
77	    message = "testing"
78	
79	    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
80	    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
81	
82	    # Make sure the timestamp differs
83	    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
84	    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
85	    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)
86

88	def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():

90	
91	    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]

94	
95	    assert item == {
96	        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
97	        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
98	        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
99	    }

from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import cast

from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
)


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    # make sure the timestamp exists and is sensible
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    # Assert the entries are what we expect
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    # Make sure the timestamp differs
    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_calls_put_item_with_serialized_item():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
                "req-123",
                "audit message",
                timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_get_audit_logger_returns_noop_logger_without_env(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.delenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, raising=False)

    logger = get_audit_logger()

    assert isinstance(logger, NoOpAuditLogger)


def test_get_audit_logger_returns_dynamodb_logger_when_env_set(
    monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch,
):
    expected = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, StubDynamoDBClient())),
    )

    def fake_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name: str) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
        return stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name, expected)

    monkeypatch.setenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "audit-table")
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.get_dynamodb_audit_logger",
        fake_get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    )

    logger = get_audit_logger()

    assert logger is expected


def test_get_dynamodb_audit_logger_builds_boto3_client(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()

    def fake_boto3_client(service_name: str) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
        return stub_boto3_client(service_name, client)

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.boto3.client",
        fake_boto3_client,
    )

    logger = get_dynamodb_audit_logger("audit-table")

    assert isinstance(logger, DynamoDBAuditLogger)
    assert logger.table_name == "audit-table"
    assert logger.dynamodb is client

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    def __init__(self, pages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: Any) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        self._last_kwargs = kwargs
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        return StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    audit.log("req-1", "some message")

    assert audit.get_logs("unknown-id") == []


# --- DynamoDBAuditLogger ---


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_calls_put_item_with_serialized_item():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
                "req-123",
                "audit message",
                timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {"id": {"S": "req-1"}, "timestamp": {"N": str(ts_ms)}, "message": {"S": "msg"}},
            ]
        }
    ]
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=pages)
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].id == "req-1"
    assert results[0].message == "msg"
    expected_ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts

    paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator = cast(
        StubDynamoDBPaginator,
        client.get_paginator("query"),
    )
    last_kwargs = paginator._last_kwargs  # pyright: ignore[reportAttributeAccessIssue]
    assert last_kwargs["KeyConditionExpression"] == "#id = :id"
    assert last_kwargs["ExpressionAttributeValues"] == {":id": {"S": "req-1"}}
    assert last_kwargs["ScanIndexForward"] is True


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_paginates():
    ts1 = 1778164245000
    ts2 = 1778164305000
    pages = [
        {"Items": [{"id": {"S": "req-1"}, "timestamp": {"N": str(ts1)}, "message": {"S": "first"}}]},
        {"Items": [{"id": {"S": "req-1"}, "timestamp": {"N": str(ts2)}, "message": {"S": "second"}}]},
    ]
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=pages)
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["first", "second"]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_returns_empty_when_no_items():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=[{"Items": []}])
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    assert logger.get_logs("req-1") == []


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_handles_page_without_items_key():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=[{}])
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    assert logger.get_logs("req-1") == []


# --- AuditEntry ---


def test_audit_entry_to_json():
    ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=ts, message="hello")

    result = entry.to_json()

    assert result == {
        "id": "req-1",
        "timestamp": "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00",
        "message": "hello",
    }


# --- S3AuditLogReader ---


def _make_stub_s3(body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None) -> Any:
    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client."""
    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

    stub = MagicMock()
    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(
            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
        )
    elif body is not None:
        response_body = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body
        stub.get_object.return_value = {"Body": response_body}
    return stub


def test_s3_reader_returns_entries_from_gzipped_jsonl():
    ts = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl([
        {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts, "message": "first"},
        {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00", "message": "second"},
    ])
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3)

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"
    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="my-bucket", Key="audit/req-1.jsonl.gz")


def test_s3_reader_uses_prefix_in_key():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl([{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}])
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="some/prefix", s3_client=s3)

    reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz")


def test_s3_reader_strips_trailing_slash_from_prefix():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl([{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}])
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="some/prefix/", s3_client=s3)

    reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz")


def test_s3_reader_empty_prefix_omits_slash():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl([{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}])
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="", s3_client=s3)

    reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(Bucket="bucket", Key="x.jsonl.gz")


def test_s3_reader_returns_empty_on_no_such_key():
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchKey")
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)

    assert reader.get_logs("missing-id") == []


def test_s3_reader_returns_empty_on_no_such_bucket():
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchBucket")
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)

    assert reader.get_logs("any-id") == []


def test_s3_reader_propagates_other_client_errors():
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="AccessDenied")
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)

    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

    with pytest.raises(ClientError):
        reader.get_logs("req-1")


def test_s3_reader_raises_on_malformed_json():
    bad_bytes = gzip.compress(b'{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00"\n{bad line')
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=bad_bytes)
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)

    with pytest.raises(json.JSONDecodeError):
        reader.get_logs("x")


# --- FallbackAuditLogReader ---


def test_fallback_reader_returns_primary_when_non_empty():
    ts = datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    primary.log("req-1", "primary entry", ts)

    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)

    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].message == "primary entry"
    fallback_s3.get_logs.assert_not_called()


def test_fallback_reader_falls_back_to_s3_when_primary_empty():
    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts = datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    expected = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=ts, message="s3 entry")]

    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)
    fallback_s3.get_logs.return_value = expected

    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert results == expected

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    def __init__(self, pages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: Any) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        self._last_kwargs = kwargs
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        return StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id():

    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }

        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
                "req-123",
                "audit message",
                timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {"id": {"S": "req-1"}, "timestamp": {"N": str(ts_ms)}, "message": {"S": "msg"}},
            ]
        }
    ]
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=pages)
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].id == "req-1"
    assert results[0].message == "msg"
    expected_ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts

    paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator = cast(
        StubDynamoDBPaginator,
        client.get_paginator("query"),
    )
    last_kwargs = paginator._last_kwargs  # pyright: ignore[reportAttributeAccessIssue]
    assert last_kwargs["KeyConditionExpression"] == "#id = :id"

def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_paginates():
    ts1 = 1778164245000

    pages = [

        {"Items": [{"id": {"S": "req-1"}, "timestamp": {"N": str(ts2)}, "message": {"S": "second"}}]},

    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["first", "second"]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_returns_empty_when_no_items():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=[{"Items": []}])

    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(

        response_body = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body
        stub.get_object.return_value = {"Body": response_body}
    return stub

from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import cast

from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,

)


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)

def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(

) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:

    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    # make sure the timestamp exists and is sensible
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    # Assert the entries are what we expect
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    # Make sure the timestamp differs
    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_calls_put_item_with_serialized_item():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
                "req-123",
                "audit message",
                timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_get_audit_logger_returns_noop_logger_without_env(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.delenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, raising=False)

    logger = get_audit_logger()

    assert isinstance(logger, NoOpAuditLogger)


def test_get_audit_logger_returns_dynamodb_logger_when_env_set(
    monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch,
):
    expected = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, StubDynamoDBClient())),
    )

    def fake_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name: str) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
        return stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name, expected)

    monkeypatch.setenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "audit-table")
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.get_dynamodb_audit_logger",
        fake_get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    )

    logger = get_audit_logger()

    assert logger is expected


def test_get_dynamodb_audit_logger_builds_boto3_client(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch):
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()

    def fake_boto3_client(service_name: str) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
        return stub_boto3_client(service_name, client)

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging.boto3.client",
        fake_boto3_client,
    )

    logger = get_dynamodb_audit_logger("audit-table")

    assert isinstance(logger, DynamoDBAuditLogger)
    assert logger.table_name == "audit-table"
    assert logger.dynamodb is client

def test_get_logs_returns_chronological_entries(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

from collections.abc import Generator
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Literal

import boto3
import pytest
from boto3.dynamodb.conditions import Key
from moto import mock_aws
from types_boto3_dynamodb.service_resource import Table

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import DynamoDBAuditLogger

TEST_TABLE_NAME: Literal["test_audit_table"] = "test_audit_table"


@pytest.fixture
def dynamodb_table() -> Generator[Table, None, None]:
    with mock_aws():
        dynamodb = boto3.resource("dynamodb", region_name="us-east-1")
        table = dynamodb.create_table(
            TableName=TEST_TABLE_NAME,
            KeySchema=[
                {"AttributeName": DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD, "KeyType": "HASH"},
                {
                    "AttributeName": DynamoDBAuditLogger.TIMESTAMP_FIELD,
                    "KeyType": "RANGE",
                },
            ],
            AttributeDefinitions=[
                {
                    "AttributeName": DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD,
                    "AttributeType": "S",
                },
                {
                    "AttributeName": DynamoDBAuditLogger.TIMESTAMP_FIELD,
                    "AttributeType": "N",
                },
            ],
            BillingMode="PAY_PER_REQUEST",
        )
        table.wait_until_exists()
        yield table


def build_audit_logger() -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    client = boto3.client("dynamodb", region_name="us-east-1")
    return DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name=TEST_TABLE_NAME,
        dynamodb=client,
    )


def test_log_persists_item_with_explicit_timestamp(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    result = dynamodb_table.get_item(
        Key={
            DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD: "req-123",
            DynamoDBAuditLogger.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000),
        }
    )

    assert "Item" in result
    item = result["Item"]
    assert item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD] == "req-123"
    assert str(item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]) == "1778164245123"
    assert item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.MESSAGE_FIELD] == "audit message"


def test_log_persists_item_with_generated_utc_timestamp(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-456", "generated timestamp message")

    after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    result = dynamodb_table.scan()

    assert "Items" in result
    items = [
        item
        for item in result["Items"]
        if item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD] == "req-456"
    ]
    assert len(items) == 1
    item = items[0]
    assert item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.MESSAGE_FIELD] == "generated timestamp message"
    timestamp_ms = int(str(item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]))
    assert timestamp_ms >= int(before.timestamp() * 1000)
    assert timestamp_ms <= int(after.timestamp() * 1000)


def test_log_relabels_non_utc_timezone_before_persisting(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)
    expected_timestamp_ms = int(
        timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc).timestamp() * 1000
    )

    logger.log("req-789", "timezone message", timestamp)

    result = dynamodb_table.get_item(
        Key={
            DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD: "req-789",
            DynamoDBAuditLogger.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: expected_timestamp_ms,
        }
    )

    assert "Item" in result
    item = result["Item"]
    assert str(item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]) == str(expected_timestamp_ms)
    assert item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.MESSAGE_FIELD] == "timezone message"


def test_log_allows_multiple_entries_for_same_id(dynamodb_table: Table):
    logger = build_audit_logger()
    first_timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    second_timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 31, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-repeat", "first message", first_timestamp)
    logger.log("req-repeat", "second message", second_timestamp)

    result = dynamodb_table.query(
        KeyConditionExpression=Key(DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD).eq("req-repeat")
    )

    assert "Items" in result
    items = result["Items"]
    assert len(items) == 2
    assert [item[DynamoDBAuditLogger.MESSAGE_FIELD] for item in items] == [
        "first message",
        "second message",
    ]

input.content:
import gzip
import json
from collections.abc import Generator
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Literal

import boto3
import pytest
from moto import mock_aws

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import S3AuditLogReader

TEST_BUCKET: Literal["test-audit-bucket"] = "test-audit-bucket"
TEST_PREFIX: Literal["audit-logs"] = "audit-logs"


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


@pytest.fixture
def s3_bucket() -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    with mock_aws():
        s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
        s3.create_bucket(
            Bucket=TEST_BUCKET,
            CreateBucketConfiguration={"LocationConstraint": "eu-west-1"},
        )
        yield


def build_reader() -> S3AuditLogReader:
    s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
    return S3AuditLogReader(bucket=TEST_BUCKET, prefix=TEST_PREFIX, s3_client=s3)


def upload(id: str, entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> None:
    s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
    key = f"{TEST_PREFIX}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
    s3.put_object(Bucket=TEST_BUCKET, Key=key, Body=_make_gzipped_jsonl(entries))


def test_get_logs_returns_entries_in_file_order(s3_bucket: None):
    ts1 = "2026-05-07T10:00:00+00:00"
    ts2 = "2026-05-07T11:00:00+00:00"
    ts3 = "2026-05-07T12:00:00+00:00"
    upload(
        "req-1",
        [
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts1, "message": "first"},
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts2, "message": "second"},
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts3, "message": "third"},
        ],
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 3
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["first", "second", "third"]


def test_get_logs_parses_timestamps_correctly(s3_bucket: None):
    ts_str = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    upload("req-ts", [{"id": "req-ts", "timestamp": ts_str, "message": "msg"}])
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-ts")

    expected_ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts


def test_get_logs_uses_prefix_in_s3_key(s3_bucket: None):
    upload("req-pfx", [{"id": "req-pfx", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}])
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-pfx")

    assert len(results) == 1


def test_get_logs_returns_empty_for_missing_object(s3_bucket: None):
    reader = build_reader()

    assert reader.get_logs("no-such-id") == []


def test_get_logs_returns_empty_for_missing_bucket():
    with mock_aws():
        s3 = boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1")
        reader = S3AuditLogReader(bucket="does-not-exist", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3)

        assert reader.get_logs("req-1") == []


def test_get_logs_ignores_blank_lines_in_jsonl(s3_bucket: None):
    lines = '{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "a"}\n\n{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T01:00:00+00:00", "message": "b"}\n'
    compressed = gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))
    boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1").put_object(
        Bucket=TEST_BUCKET, Key=f"{TEST_PREFIX}/req-1.jsonl.gz", Body=compressed
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["a", "b"]

        assert reader.get_logs("req-1") == []


def test_get_logs_ignores_blank_lines_in_jsonl(s3_bucket: None):
    lines = '{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "a"}\n\n{"id": "req-1", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T01:00:00+00:00", "message": "b"}\n'
    compressed = gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))
    boto3.client("s3", region_name="eu-west-1").put_object(
        Bucket=TEST_BUCKET, Key=f"{TEST_PREFIX}/req-1.jsonl.gz", Body=compressed
    )
    reader = build_reader()

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["a", "b"]

input.content:
import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
)
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.request_repository import REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR

# Set required env vars before reporter_lambda is imported — its module-level
# get_audit_log_reader() call needs these to be present at import time.
_ = os.environ.setdefault(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE, "test-audit-table")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET, "test-bucket")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "test-prefix")
_ = os.environ.setdefault(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR, "memory")

import pytest  # noqa: E402

import dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda as reporter_module  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import AuditEntry, FallbackAuditLogReader  # noqa: E402
from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.reporter_lambda import lambda_handler  # noqa: E402


def make_get_event(id: str | None = "req-123") -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "pathParameters": {"id": id} if id is not None else None,
        "httpMethod": "GET",
    }


def make_mock_reader(entries: list[AuditEntry] | Exception) -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
    mock = MagicMock(spec=FallbackAuditLogReader)
    if isinstance(entries, Exception):
        mock.get_logs.side_effect = entries
    else:
        mock.get_logs.return_value = entries
    return mock


def make_context() -> MagicMock:
    ctx = MagicMock()
    return ctx


TS = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def test_lambda_handler_returns_200_with_entries(monkeypatch):
    entries = [
        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=TS, message="created"),
        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=datetime(2026, 5, 7, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc), message="processed"),
    ]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 200
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-123"
    assert len(body["entries"]) == 2
    assert body["entries"][0]["message"] == "created"
    assert body["entries"][1]["message"] == "processed"
    assert body["entries"][0]["timestamp"] == "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"


def test_lambda_handler_returns_404_when_no_entries(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-unknown"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 404
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["id"] == "req-unknown"
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_path_param_missing(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {"pathParameters": None}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert "error" in body


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_id_is_empty_string(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("  "), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_400_when_pathparameters_key_absent(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
    event: dict[str, object] = {}

    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 400


def test_lambda_handler_returns_500_on_reader_exception(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(RuntimeError("dynamo down"))
    )

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())

    assert response["statusCode"] == 500
    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["error"] == "internal error"


def test_lambda_handler_response_has_json_content_type(monkeypatch):
    entries = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="msg")]
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    assert response["headers"] == {"Content-Type": "application/json"}  # type: ignore[index]


def test_lambda_handler_entry_shape_matches_audit_entry_to_json(monkeypatch):
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=TS, message="test msg")
    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([entry]))

    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-1"), make_context())

    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert body["entries"][0] == entry.to_json()

25	)  # noqa: E402

27	
28	
29	def make_get_event(id: str | None = "req-123") -> dict[str, object]:
30	    return {
31	        "pathParameters": {"id": id} if id is not None else None,
32	        "httpMethod": "GET",
33	    }
34	
35	
36	def make_mock_reader(entries: list[AuditEntry] | Exception) -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
37	    mock = MagicMock(spec=FallbackAuditLogReader)
38	    if isinstance(entries, Exception):
39	        mock.get_logs.side_effect = entries
40	    else:
41	        mock.get_logs.return_value = entries
42	    return mock
43	
44	
45	def make_context() -> MagicMock:
46	    ctx = MagicMock()
47	    return ctx
48	
49	
50	TS = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
51	
52	
53	def test_lambda_handler_returns_200_with_entries(monkeypatch):
54	    entries = [
55	        AuditEntry(id="req-123", timestamp=TS, message="created"),
56	        AuditEntry(
57	            id="req-123",
58	            timestamp=datetime(2026, 5, 7, 15, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
59	            message="processed",
60	        ),
61	    ]
62	    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader(entries))
63	
64	    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-123"), make_context())
65	
66	    assert response["statusCode"] == 200
67	    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
68	    assert body["id"] == "req-123"
69	    assert len(body["entries"]) == 2
70	    assert body["entries"][0]["message"] == "created"
71	    assert body["entries"][1]["message"] == "processed"
72	    assert body["entries"][0]["timestamp"] == "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
73	
74	
75	def test_lambda_handler_returns_404_when_no_entries(monkeypatch):
76	    monkeypatch.setattr(reporter_module, "reader", make_mock_reader([]))
77	
78	    response = lambda_handler(make_get_event("req-unknown"), make_context())
79	
80	    assert response["statusCode"] == 404
81	    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
82	    assert body["id"] == "req-unknown"
83	    assert "error" in body
84	
85

89	
90	    response = lambda_handler(event, make_context())
91	
92	    assert response["statusCode"] == 400
93	    body = json.loads(response["body"])  # type: ignore[arg-type]
94	    assert "error" in body
95	
96

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of everything implemented:

---

## What was built

**New files:**
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/reporter_lambda.py` — Lambda handler for `GET /status/{id}`. Returns 200 with entries, 404 if no logs found, 400 if id is missing, 500 on error.
- `tests/test_s3_audit_log_reader.py` — moto-based integration tests for the S3 reader.
- `tests/test_reporter_lambda.py` — handler unit tests covering all response codes and edge cases.

**Modified files:**
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py` — Added `AuditEntry` dataclass, `get_logs()` abstract method + impls on all loggers, `S3AuditLogReader` (reads `{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz`, handles `NoSuchKey`/`NoSuchBucket` gracefully), `FallbackAuditLogReader` (tries DynamoDB first, falls back to S3 when empty), and factory helpers `get_s3_audit_log_reader()` / `get_audit_log_reader()`.
- `tests/test_audit_logging.py` — Added ~60 lines of new unit tests for all new classes and factories.
- `tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` — Added 3 integration tests for `get_logs()`.
- `template.yaml` — Added `AuditLogS3Bucket`/`AuditLogS3Prefix` parameters, `ReporterServerlessAccessManager`, `ReporterS3ReadPolicy` (IAM), `ReporterFunction`, and `/status/{id}` route on `CreateRequestApi`. Added `GetStatusApiInvokeUrl` output.
- `samconfig.toml` — Added S3 bucket/prefix values for dev and prod.
- `Makefile` — Added `build-ReporterFunction` target.
- `TODO.md` — Marked retrieval sub-bullets done.

**Results:** 134 tests passing, 0 ruff errors, 0 black violations, 0 type errors, `sam validate --lint` clean, `sam build` succeeded for all 3 functions.

> **Reminder:** The `AuditLogS3Bucket` values in `samconfig.toml` are placeholders — confirm the actual bucket names with the infra team before deploying.

- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.new_string:
## Refactor: consolidate field-name constants onto `AuditEntry`

Right now the audit field names (`"id"`, `"timestamp"`, `"message"`) are duplicated:
- `DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD/TIMESTAMP_FIELD/MESSAGE_FIELD` (`audit_logging.py:110-112`) for DynamoDB attribute names.
- Hard-coded literal strings inside `AuditEntry.to_json` (`audit_logging.py:31-36`) and `S3AuditLogReader.get_logs` (`audit_logging.py:206-213`) — these JSON keys must match the DynamoDB names so the S3 materialiser and reader stay symmetric.

Move the three constants onto `AuditEntry` as the single source of truth:

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AuditEntry:
    ID_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["id"]] = "id"
    TIMESTAMP_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["timestamp"]] = "timestamp"
    MESSAGE_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["message"]] = "message"

    id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    message: str

    def to_json(self) -> dict[str, str]:
        return {
            self.ID_FIELD: self.id,
            self.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: self.timestamp.isoformat(),
            self.MESSAGE_FIELD: self.message,
        }

    @classmethod
    def from_json(cls, data: dict[str, str]) -> "AuditEntry":
        return cls(
            id=data[cls.ID_FIELD],
            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(data[cls.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]),
            message=data[cls.MESSAGE_FIELD],
        )
```

`ClassVar` keeps these out of the dataclass field set so they don't become constructor args or affect equality — only instance fields (`id`, `timestamp`, `message`) participate in the dataclass machinery.

Then update call sites to read from `AuditEntry` instead of `DynamoDBAuditLogger`:

| File | Lines | Change |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `audit_logging.py` | 110-112 | **Delete** the three `*_FIELD` class attrs from `DynamoDBAuditLogger` |
| `audit_logging.py` | 139, 155-157, 163-165 | Replace `self.ID_FIELD`/`cls.ID_FIELD` etc. with `AuditEntry.ID_FIELD`/`AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD`/`AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD` |
| `audit_logging.py` | 206-213 | `S3AuditLogReader.get_logs` uses `AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(line))` instead of indexing literal `"id"`/`"timestamp"`/`"message"` |
| `tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` | 23, 25, 31, 35, 61, 62, 68-70, 86, 90, 91, 108, 109, 115, 116, 128, 134 | Replace `DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD` etc. with `AuditEntry.ID_FIELD` etc. |
| `tests/test_audit_logging.py` | new tests added in feature 1 | Use `AuditEntry.*_FIELD` directly; existing tests do not reference these constants |

`DynamoDBRequestRepository` (`request_repository.py`) has its own unrelated `ID_FIELD` constant for the requests table — it stays as is. The audit table constants are the only ones moving.

This refactor lands as part of feature 1 because the new `S3AuditLogReader` and `AuditEntry.to_json` immediately benefit (the JSONL keys on S3 must equal the DynamoDB attribute names).

### Verification for the refactor

- `uv run pytest` — all existing audit logger tests must pass unchanged.
- `uv run prek` — basedpyright must accept `ClassVar[Literal["id"]]`.
- Spot-check that no test accidentally relies on `DynamoDBAuditLogger.ID_FIELD` being a class attribute on `DynamoDBAuditLogger` itself (the grep above lists all references — only the test file referenced needs updating).

## Open items to confirm before merging
